rationale: Ground truth here is empty of substantive concerns (two LGTM approvals, no inline comments), so recall is vacuously satisfied by both. Both candidates independently surface the same real latent gap — stale hardcoded '3' lower-bound assertions in tests/dfx/reliability/invalid_param_test/{test_invalid_image_editing,test_invalid_image_generation}.py that will break once layers=2 becomes valid — and the near-identical file/line convergence (65, 90-91) gives strong confidence the finding is grounded rather than fabricated. X is slightly more exhaustive in its repo-wide grep sweeps and adds a minor boundary-test suggestion, but bundles the generation.py:91 issue in with hedged 'if present' language. Y is more actionable (shows exact before/after code per issue, cleanly separates all three broken lines) and does deeper functional validation of the pipeline code confirming layers=2 is genuinely supported, though its closing note about the author 'disclosing' an unrun H100 regression is an unverifiable/likely-incorrect aside given spencerr221's comment confirming the L4 regression (including layers_guard_001_layers2) already passed.
